ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Ama, di i wa la n nɛra be ba ga kpollii waa, nua la hiŋ ŋɔwɛɛ di hɛɛhzi la wàá dii.’
translated: "My friend, it seems that the wolf has come again, so let us quickly hide ourselves in the bushes."

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: N daŋ kpa Keenan hɛri naa iin duwɛɛ di ɛɛ hɔɔhra naa maa, tɛɛ i ni i naanna maa, di hanna la, di jaa ɛɛ Kɔraawiizi la ban ninna hum.”
translated: "Keenan here now with two new guests, one from Ethiopia and the other from Kenya. They are here to discuss the Korawa tribe in detail."

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Wiizi faa futtɔɔ di ra tinni mɔɔ ka Lɔt haa hel a bol.
translated: The woman who sells fruits at the market in Lot has a big basket.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: U hahjiga Rema biibaala genii faa, Tɛba, Gaham, Tahas ani Maaka.
translated: You are from the Rema tribe, Teba, Gaham, Tahas, and Maaka.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Awia, Lɔt lizi Jɔɔdan Waluu la, di izi kaali wiifurii. Gunnɛ ba nɛra banɛɛ faa bargi dɔŋa ni.
translated: The old man from Jɔɔdan Waluu village is very sick. He needs urgent medical help.

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Aizik pɛɛzuu, “Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ di nau manaŋ kennei?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la hinna hum labuu hiiniŋ.”
translated: Jekob replied, "We have already lost our home."

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Denii Jekɔb la ga faamuu. Uŋ ga faamuu la, u nuu u laala la sɔɔh, ka faa jaa kissuu di ŋɔ, “N biibaal sɔɔh summɔɔ alugɔ pir yembɛl Kɔraawiizi n kisseere.
translated: Their Jacob has died. And when they heard, they were greatly distressed, as if they had been struck by a heavy blow, and said, "Indeed, a great calamity has befallen us, the people of Korawizi, and we are filled with sorrow."

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Denii Keenan mɛɛ Ham nau, di zum lugɔ u mɛɛ chɔɔwɔɔ ni chakpal. U lii bossee di ga kali u kpɛɛnna gunla un nawɛɛ.
translated: Denii Keenan has not come yet, but the time for the meeting is approaching. He will probably bring some new guns with him.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Hin niŋ luluu la, u daŋ ɛɛ lugɔ kaukpoŋlii, u ni nɛr maa wàá dɔɔ nuukpaŋ. U ni u lulla gba wàá dɔɔ nuukpaŋ.”
translated: "You are not a child anymore, you should stop playing and start working seriously. You should help your father in the farm."

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Disɔn, Ɛza ani Disan, di lii ba dizi biini, Ɛdɔm hɛri. Ɛdɔm koora wia
translated: "Dison, Ezra and Disan, they are the ones who live here, in the forest. They are the ones who play the drums."
